explanation: The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1 (standby)" means that whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must also be true. The word "shall" indicates that this condition must hold globally. Therefore, the LTL translation is G(prop_1 -> prop_0).

The phrase "A shall occur when B" means that whenever B is true, A must also be true at that same instant. This is a global implication. Thus, "The autopilot shall prop_0 when the prop_1 (standby)" translates to "Globally, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must be true."

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1" means that at any point in time, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must also be true. The "shall" indicates a global requirement. This translates to a global implication: G (prop_1 -> prop_0).
The phrase "A shall B when C" translates to "Globally (if C is true, then B must be true)". In this case, 'prop_0' is B and 'prop_1' is C, so it becomes G (prop_1 -> prop_0).

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when the prop_1 (standby)" implies a continuous condition: whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must also be true at that exact moment. This type of universal requirement across all time steps is best captured by the Global (G) operator, applied to an implication (->). Thus, G (prop_1 -> prop_0) means 'Globally, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must be true'.

The phrase "when prop_1" indicates that `prop_1` is a condition. The statement implies that at any point in time, if `prop_1` is true, then `prop_0` must also be true. The 'G' (globally) operator ensures this implication holds at all times.

The phrase "A when B" typically translates to a global implication: G (B -> A). In this case, A is 'prop_0' and B is 'prop_1'. Thus, if 'prop_1' is true, 'prop_0' must also be true, and this relationship must hold globally.

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1" indicates a global requirement. It means that at any point in time, if 'prop_1' is true, then 'prop_0' must also be true at that same point in time. This is best represented by a global implication.

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when the prop_1 (standby)" means that if prop_1 is true at any point, then prop_0 must also be true at that same point. This is a global implication, as it must hold true at all times.

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1" means that whenever `prop_1` is true, `prop_0` must also be true at that same instant. This is a continuous implication, best represented by `G (prop_1 -> prop_0)`, which states that globally, if `prop_1` is true, it implies `prop_0` is true.
